verdict_reasoning: LAMP3 is a type 1 integral membrane protein that localizes primarily to late endosomes/lysosomes and MHC class II compartments—classically intracellular. However, in mature dendritic cells, LAMP3-containing compartments traffic to the cell surface via lysosomal exocytosis, where it serves as a maturation marker (CD208). The protein body reaches the outer leaflet through fusion of late-endosomal/lysosomal vesicles with the plasma membrane during dendritic cell activation and antigen presentation. This surface mobilization is documented and condition-dependent, not baseline.
